narration: The average American blows thirty-one thousand dollars on regretted impulse buys every single decade. That's not a willpower problem — it's a brain problem. And one simple rule fixes it.
visual: Dark background. Amber person silhouette surrounded by floating white price tags ($47, $89, $124, $220) drifting upward. '$31,000' in large amber text at top with 'IMPULSE BUY TRAP' subtitle.

narration: Your brain floods with dopamine the moment you see something you want — not when you actually buy it. That rush fades on its own within seventy-two hours. You don't need willpower, you need time.
visual: Light background. Dark head silhouette with amber brain pulsing inside. Dopamine sparks radiate outward. Clock below shows '72 HRS' with hand sweeping. Text: YOUR BRAIN WANTS IT NOW.

narration: The rule is dead simple. Never buy anything non-essential over thirty dollars in the moment. Write it down. Wait seventy-two hours. Then decide.
visual: Dark background. Green $30 bill with red X crossing it out. Three checklist items spring in one by one: 'NON-ESSENTIAL?', 'OVER $30?', 'WAIT 72 HOURS'. Amber '72 HOURS' at bottom.

narration: People who use the rule cancel eighty-seven percent of impulse purchases after waiting. On a hundred-dollar item, that's eighty-seven dollars back in your pocket every single time.
visual: Light background. Two-bar chart animates up: tall amber bar (100 impulse buys) vs short green bar (13 actually needed). '87% NEVER BOUGHT' label. Percentage counter grows to 87.

narration: The trick is keeping a 'someday list' on your phone instead of adding things to a cart. Most items fall off the list by themselves within a week — without you spending a cent.
visual: Dark background. Phone outline with 'SOMEDAY LIST' on screen, three items appearing one by one, third item crossed out. Pink piggy bank beside phone with coin dropping in repeatedly.

narration: Next time you want to click 'buy now,' add it to the list instead. Give it seventy-two hours. That one pause saves you thirty-one thousand dollars this decade. Start the list tonight.
visual: Light background. Phone showing someday list with pulsing amber 'ADD TO LIST' button. Savings counter grows from $0 to $31,000. Bold 'START TONIGHT' in black at bottom.
